title: FLF: DNS C2 Pattern Analysis
description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for DNS command-and-control patterns: long or suspicious DNS query names, dynamic DNS infrastructure, high-risk TLDs, and repeated queries by host.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and 'Event ID' = '22' and 'Query Name' != null and ('Query Name' like '*.dnsexfil.example' or 'Query Name' like '*.duckdns.org' or 'Query Name' like '*.xyz' or 'Query Name' like '*.top' or 'Query Name' like '*.info' or 'Query Name' like '*.cc' or 'Query Name' like '*.pw' or 'Query Name' like '*.tk') | stats count as DNSQueries, distinctcount('Query Name') as UniqueQueries, unique('Query Name') as QueryNames by 'Host Name (Server)' | where DNSQueries > 2 | sort -DNSQueries

ask_ai_prompts: Which host has DNS query patterns most consistent with DNS C2?
List the suspicious query names and explain whether the activity looks like beaconing or exfiltration.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Domain Name System As C2
